| Microsoft Access 2016\Application Settings\Security\Trust Center\Trusted Locations | Trusted Location #8 | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\security\trusted locations\location8!path HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\security\trusted locations\location8!date HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\security\trusted locations\location8!description HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\security\trusted locations\location8!allowsubfolders | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to specify a location that is used as a trusted source for opening files in this application.  Files in trusted locations bypass file validation active content checks and Protected View. Macros and code in these files will execute without displaying warnings to the user. If you change or add a location make sure that the new location is secured with only appropriate user permissions to add document/files.If you enable this policy setting you may specify a folder location path and date from which files can the application can open files which run macros without warning.  If you check the "Allow sub folders" check box then all sub-folders in the folder you specify will also be trusted.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting the trusted location is not specified. |

Macros and code in these files will execute without displaying warnings to the user. If you change or add a location make sure that the new location is secured with only appropriate user permissions to add document/files.If you enable this policy setting you may specify a folder location path and date from which files can the application can open files which run macros without warning.  If you check the "Allow sub folders" check box then all sub-folders in the folder you specify will also be trusted.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting the trusted location is not specified. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Application Settings\Web Options...\General | Followed hyperlink color | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\internet!followedhyperlinkcolor | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Specifies the default text color of followed hyperlinks. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Application Settings\Web Options...\General | Hyperlink color | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\internet!hyperlinkcolor | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Specifies the default hyperlink text color. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Application Settings\Web Options...\General | Underline hyperlinks | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\internet!donotunderlinehyperlinks | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting controls whether hyperlinks in Access tables queries forms and reports are underlined.       If you enable this policy setting Access underlines all hyperlinks in tables queries forms and reports when they are created overriding any configuration changes on the users' computers.       If you disable this policy setting Access does not underline hyperlinks in tables queries forms and reports.       If you do not configure this policy setting Access underlines hyperlinks that appear in tables queries forms and reports.       Enabling this policy setting enforces the default configuration in Access and is therefore unlikely to cause a significant usability issue for most users. If this configuration is changed users might click on dangerous hyperlinks without realizing it which could pose a security risk. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Customizable Error Messages | List of error messages to customize | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\customizablealerts!policyon HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\customizablealerts | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Defines a list of custom error messages to activate. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Disable Items in User Interface\Custom | Disable commands | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledcmdbaritemslist | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to disable any command bar button and menu item with a command bar ID including command bar buttons and menu items that are not in the predefined lists.If you enable this policy setting you can enter an ID number to disable a specific command bar button or menu item. The ID number needs to be in decimal (not hexadecimal).  Multiple values should be separated by commas.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting all default command bar buttons or menu items are available to users. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Disable Items in User Interface\Custom | Disable shortcut keys | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledshortcutkeyslist | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to disable any shortcut key by using its virtual key code ID including shortcut keys that are not in the predefined lists.If you enable this policy setting you can enter a virtual key code ID number to disable a specific shortcut key.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting all default shortcut keys are enabled for users. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Disable Items in User Interface\Predefined | Disable predefined commands | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledcmdbaritemscheckboxes!sendtomailrecipient HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledcmdbaritemscheckboxes!toolssecuritysetpwd HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledcmdbaritemscheckboxes!toolssecurityperms HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledcmdbaritemscheckboxes!toolssecurityaccts HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledcmdbaritemscheckboxes!toolssecuritysecwiz HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledcmdbaritemscheckboxes!toolssecurityencrypt HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledcmdbaritemscheckboxes!toolsmacrovbe HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledcmdbaritemscheckboxes!accrunmacro HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledcmdbaritemscheckboxes!toolsmacroconvertmacros HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledcmdbaritemscheckboxes!toolsmacrocreateshortcutmenu HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledcmdbaritemscheckboxes!addinsmenuicon | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to disable specific command bar buttons and menu items in the specified applications.       If you enable this policy setting you can disable specific command bar buttons and menu items in the user interface for the selected application.  The predefined list of command bar buttons and menu items you can disable becomes available to you when you enable this policy setting.       If you disable or do not configure this policy setting the predefined list of command bar buttons and menu items are enabled for the application. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Disable Items in User Interface\Predefined | Disable shortcut keys | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledshortcutkeyscheckboxes!find HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledshortcutkeyscheckboxes!inserthyperlink HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\disabledshortcutkeyscheckboxes!toolsmacrovbe | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to disable specific shortcut key combinations in the specified applications.       If you enable this policy setting you can disable specific shortcut keys for the selected application.  The predefined list of shortcut keys you can disable becomes available to you when you enable this policy setting.       If you disable or do not configure this policy setting the predefined list of shortcut keys are enabled for the application. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Miscellaneous | Block all unmanaged add-ins | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\resiliency!restricttolist | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting blocks all add-ins that are not managed by the "List of managed add-ins" policy setting.If you enable this policy setting and the "List of managed add-ins" policy setting is also enabled all add-ins are blocked except those that are configured as 1 (always enabled) or 2 (configurable by the user) in the "List of managed add-ins" policy setting.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting users can enable or disable any add-ins that are not managed by the "List of managed add-ins" policy setting. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Miscellaneous | Clear cache on close | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\settings!clearcacheonclose | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to force the cache to be cleared for databases that are not compatible with Access 2016 when the database is closed.If you enable this policy setting then any cached Microsoft SharePoint or Business Data Catalog (BDC) links will be cleared on close for databases that do not use Access 2016 compatible caching.  If the database uses Access 2016 compatible caching then this policy setting will have no effect when enabled.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting then any cached Microsoft SharePoint or Business Data Catalog (BDC) links will be not cleared on close for databases that do not use Access 2016 compatible caching.  If the database uses Access 2016 compatible caching then this policy setting will have no effect when disabled or not configured. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Miscellaneous | Default file format | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\settings!default file format | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting controls whether new database files are created in the new Access format or in a format used by earlier versions of Access.      If you enable this policy setting you can specify whether new database files are created in Access 2016 format by default or in Access 2002--2003 format. Users can still override the default and select a specific format when they save the files but cannot set the default by themselves from the Access Options dialog.       If you disable or do not configure this policy setting when users create new database files Access saves them in the new Access 2016 format; however users can change this functionality by selecting a file format from the Default file format drop down list under Access Options | Popular | Creating databases. Note: If you disable this policy setting users can choose from three default file formats: Access 2000 Access 2002--2003 and Access 2016. You can use this policy setting to specify either the Access 2002--2003 or Access 2016 format as the default but not the Access 2000 format. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Miscellaneous | Do not prompt to convert older databases | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\settings!noconvertdialog | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting controls whether Access prompts users to convert older databases when they are opened.       If you enable this policy setting Access will leave Access 97-format databases unchanged. Access informs the user that the database is in the older format but does not provide the user with an option to convert the database. Some features introduced in more recent versions of Access will not be available and the user will not be able to make any design changes to the database.       If you disable or do not configure this policy setting when users open databases that were created in the Access 97 file format Access prompts them to convert the database to a newer file format. Users can choose to convert the database or leave it in the older format. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Miscellaneous | List of managed add-ins | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\resiliency\addinlist!policyon HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\resiliency\addinlist | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to specify which add-ins are always enabled always disabled (blocked) or configurable by the user. To block add-ins that are not managed by this policy setting you must also configure the "Block all unmanaged add-ins" policy setting.To enable this policy setting provide the following information for each add-in:In "Value name" specify the programmatic identifier (ProgID) for COM add-ins.To obtain the ProgID for an add-in use Registry Editor on the client computer where the add-in is installed to locate key names under HKEY\_LOCAL\_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Office\Access\Addins or HKEY\_CURRENT\_USER\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Office\Access\Addins.You can also obtain the ProgID of an add-in by using Office Telemetry Dashboard.In "Value" specify the value as follows:To specify that an add-in is always disabled (blocked) type 0.To specify that an add-in is always enabled type 1.To specify that an add-in is configurable by the user and not blocked by the "Block all unmanaged add-ins" policy setting when enabled type 2.If you disable or do not enable this policy setting the list of managed add-ins is deleted. If the "Block all unmanaged add-ins" policy setting is enabled then all add-ins are blocked. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Miscellaneous | Never cache data | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\settings!nevercache | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to force databases that do not use Access 2016 compatible caching to not cache any data.If you enable this policy setting then data from Microsoft SharePoint or Business Data Catalog (BDC) will not be cached for databases that do not use Access 2016 compatible caching.  If the database uses Access 2016 compatible caching then this policy setting will have no effect when enabled.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting then data from Microsoft SharePoint or Business Data Catalog (BDC) will be cached for databases that do not use Access 2016 compatible caching.  If the database uses Access 2016 compatible caching then this policy setting will have no effect when disabled or not configured. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Miscellaneous | Personal templates path for Access | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\settings!personaltemplatesaccess | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting specifies the location of a user's personal templates. If you enable this policy setting users will see any templates they have saved in the specified location in the custom templates tab on the Office Start screen and in File | New and when saving a template their default folder will change to be the specified location. If you disable or do not configure this policy setting users will not see templates they have saved in the custom templates tab on the Office Start screen and in File | New and when saving a template their default folder will be their document save location. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Miscellaneous | Show custom templates tab by default in Access on the Office Start screen and in File | New | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\settings!officestartdefaulttab | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting controls whether custom templates (when they exist) show as the default tab in Access on the Office Start screen and in File | New. If you enable this policy setting users will the see custom templates tab as the default tab in Access on the Office Start screen and in File | New when templates exist (this can include Custom XML programmed templates templates in the Workgroup templates path templates in the Personal templates path or SharePoint templates).If you disable or do not configure this policy setting users will see the Featured templates tab as the default tab in Access on the Office Start screen and in File | New unless all Office-provided templates have been disabled. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Miscellaneous | Use Access 2007 compatible cache | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\settings!use2007compatiblecache | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to force new and existing databases to use a cache compatible Access 2007.If you enable this policy setting new and existing databases will use caching compatible with Access 2007.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting new databases will default to use caching that is not compatible with Access 2007.  Existing databases will use the caching mode that they were created with. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Tools | Security | Modal Trust Decision Only | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\security!modaltrustdecisiononly | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting controls how Access notifies users about untrusted components.       If you enable this policy setting when users attempt to open an untrusted Access database that contains user-programmed executable components users see a dialog box where they then must choose whether to enable or disable the components before they can work with the database.       If you disable or do not configure this policy setting when users open an untrusted Access database that contains user-programmed executable components Access opens the database with the components disabled and displays the Message Bar with a warning that database content has been disabled. Users can inspect the contents of the database but cannot use any disabled functionality until they enable it by clicking Options on the Message Bar and selecting the appropriate action. |
| Microsoft Access 2016\Tools | Security\Workgroup Administrator... | Path to shared Workgroup information file for secured MDB files | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\access\access connectivity engine\engines!systemdb | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Specifies the default path and filename for the workgroup information file. |
| Microsoft Excel 2016\Customizable Error Messages | List of error messages to customize | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\excel\customizablealerts!policyon HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\excel\customizablealerts | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Enter error ID for Value Name and custom button text for Value |
| Microsoft Excel 2016\Data Recovery | Do not show data extraction options when opening corrupt workbooks | Enabled | Security Baseline | File handling | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\excel\options!extractdatadisableui | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting controls whether Excel presents users with a list of data extraction options before beginning an Open and Repair operation when users choose to open a corrupt workbook in repair or extract mode.      If you enable this policy setting Excel opens the file using the Safe Load process and does not prompt users to choose between repairing or extracting data.      If you disable or do not configure this policy setting Excel prompts the user to select either to repair or to extract data and to select either to convert to values or to recover formulas. |

The ID number needs to be in decimal (not hexadecimal).  Multiple values should be separated by commas.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting all default command bar buttons or menu items are available to users. |
