| Microsoft Office 2016\File Open/Save dialog box\Restricted Browsing | Activate Restricted Browsing | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\open find\restrictedbrowse\optin!microsoft access HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\open find\restrictedbrowse\optin!microsoft excel HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\open find\restrictedbrowse\optin!microsoft sharepoint designer HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\open find\restrictedbrowse\optin!microsoft infopath HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\open find\restrictedbrowse\optin!microsoft onenote HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\open find\restrictedbrowse\optin!microsoft outlook HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\open find\restrictedbrowse\optin!microsoft powerpoint HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\open find\restrictedbrowse\optin!microsoft project HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\open find\restrictedbrowse\optin!microsoft publisher HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\open find\restrictedbrowse\optin!microsoft visio HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\open find\restrictedbrowse\optin!microsoft word | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | When Restricted Browsing is activated the save as dialog box will be restricted such that the user will only be able to navigate to those locations and the children of those locations specified in the "Restricted Browsing\Approve Locations" policy setting.  If you want to enable the "Approve Locations" policy setting you must first enable the "Approve Locations" policy setting first. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\File Open/Save dialog box\Restricted Browsing | Approve Locations | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\open find\restrictedbrowse | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Adds locations such as c:\Windows or \\server\share to the list of approved locations for use with Restricted Browsing. When Restricted Browsing is active the Save As dialog box is restricted such that the user can navigate only to the locations and the children of the locations specified in this list. To allow easier access to these approved locations consider adding them to the Places bar by using the Places Bar Locations setting for the File Open/Save dialog box. If there are no approved locations in the Places bar the dialog box may not be able to open.To activate Restricted Browsing use the Restricted Browsing/Activate Restricted Browsing setting.  Note: You must set this policy setting first before the "Activate Restricted Browsing". |
| Microsoft Office 2016\First Run | Disable First Run Movie | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\firstrun!disablemovie | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting determines whether a video about signing-in to Office is played when Office first runs.If you enable this policy setting the video does not run during Office First Run.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting a video about signing-in to Office plays when Office first runs. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\First Run | Disable Office First Run on application boot | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\firstrun!bootedrtm | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting determines whether the Office First Run comes up on first application boot if not previously viewed.If you enable this policy setting  the Office First Run does not run on first application boot.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting the Office First Run about signing-in to Office comes up on first application boot if not previously viewed. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize | Allow roaming of all user customizations | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars!customuiroaming | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows roaming of both the Quick Access Toolbar and Ribbon customizations.  If you enable this policy setting users' Quick Access Toolbar and Ribbon customizations will be available to them on any computer on their network when they log on. If you disable or do not configure this policy setting users' Quick Access Toolbar and Ribbon customizations will only be available to them on the computer on which they made the customizations. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize | Default Office theme | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common!default ui theme | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to select the user interface (UI) theme used by Office if the user has not already selected an Office theme.Note: This setting only applies to Version 1903 or later of Office.If you enable this policy setting you may choose the Office theme used in cases where the user has not selected an Office theme themselves.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting Office will use the Colorful theme in cases where the user has not selected an Office theme themselves.Regardless of how you configure this policy setting users can change their Office theme by going to File > Account > Office Theme (or in Outlook by going to File > Office Account > Office Theme).Note: The “Default Office theme” policy setting located under Computer Configuration takes precedence over this policy setting. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize | Disable UI extending from documents and templates | Disallow in Word\tEnabled\nDisallow in Excel\tEnabled\nDisallow in PowerPoint\tEnabled\nDisallow in Access\tEnabled\nDisallow in Outlook\tEnabled\nDisallow in Publisher\tEnabled\nDisallow in Project\tDisabled\nDisallow in Visio\tDisabled\nDisallow in InfoPath\tEnabled | Security Baseline | Add-ins and Extensibility | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\word!noextensibilitycustomizationfromdocument HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\excel!noextensibilitycustomizationfromdocument HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\powerpoint!noextensibilitycustomizationfromdocument HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\access!noextensibilitycustomizationfromdocument HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\outlook!noextensibilitycustomizationfromdocument HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\publisher!noextensibilitycustomizationfromdocument HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\project!noextensibilitycustomizationfromdocument HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\visio!noextensibilitycustomizationfromdocument HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\infopath!noextensibilitycustomizationfromdocument | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting controls whether Office 2016 applications load any custom user interface (UI) code included with a document or template.  Office 2016 allows developers to extend the UI with customization code that is included in a document or template. If you enable this policy setting Office 2016 applications cannot load any UI customization code included with documents and templates. If you disable or do not configure this policy setting Office 2016 applications load any UI customization code included with a document or template when opening it. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize | Do not show ScreenTips on toolbars | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars!tooltips | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to configure the "Show ScreenTips on Toolbars" option.If you enable this policy setting ScreenTips will not be shown on toolbars.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting ScreenTips will be shown on toolbars. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize | Large icons | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars!btnsize | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Checks/Unchecks the corresponding UI option.  This option only applies to CommandBars UI. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize | List font names in their font | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars!fontview | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Checks/Unchecks the corresponding UI option.  This option only applies to CommandBars UI. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize | Menu animations | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars!animation | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Checks/Unchecks the corresponding UI option.  This option only applies to CommandBars UI. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize | Show shortcut keys in ScreenTips | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars!showkbdshortcuts | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Checks/Unchecks the corresponding UI option. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize | Turn off all user customizations | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\word!nousercustomization HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\excel!nousercustomization HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\powerpoint!nousercustomization HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\access!nousercustomization HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\outlook!nousercustomization HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\sharepoint designer!nousercustomization HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\publisher!nousercustomization HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\project!nousercustomization HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\visio!nousercustomization HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\onenote!nousercustomization HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\infopath!nousercustomization HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\groove!nousercustomization | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting can prevent users from making any Quick Access Toolbar and the Ribbon customizations.  This includes customizations made through user interface (UI) entry points or loaded from documents or templates.If you enable this policy setting users will not be able to customize the Quick Access Toolbar and Ribbon through either the Quick Access Toolbar and Ribbon tabs in the application's Office Center dialog box or the right-click menu on the Ribbon.  In addition Quick Access Toolbar and Ribbon customizations originating from documents or templates will not be loaded when these documents are opened.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting users can make Quick Access Toolbar and Ribbon customizations through the UI as well as load them from documents and templates. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize | Turn off user customizations via UI | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\word!disabletoolbarcustomizationui HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\excel!disabletoolbarcustomizationui HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\powerpoint!disabletoolbarcustomizationui HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\access!disabletoolbarcustomizationui HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\outlook!disabletoolbarcustomizationui HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\sharepoint designer!disabletoolbarcustomizationui HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\publisher!disabletoolbarcustomizationui HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\project!disabletoolbarcustomizationui HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\visio!disabletoolbarcustomizationui HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\onenote!disabletoolbarcustomizationui HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\infopath!msoriddisabletoolbarcustomizationuiinfopath HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\toolbars\groove!disabletoolbarcustomizationui | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting can prevent users from customizing both the Quick Access Toolbar and Ribbon through the user interface (UI).If you enable this policy setting the following UI entry points are turned off: the Quick Access Toolbar and Ribbon tabs in the application's Office Center dialog box and the Quick Access Toolbar and Ribbon customization options on the right-click menu on the Ribbon.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting users can customize the Quick Access Toolbar and Ribbon through both the application’s Office Center dialog box and the right-click menu on the Ribbon. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize\Shared Workspace | Automatic Discovery | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\sharepointtracking!automaticdiscovery | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Enables/Disables the Automatic Discovery feature. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize\Shared Workspace | Disable user from setting personal site as default location | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\portal!disablesetpersonalsite | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Checked: User is not able to define the default location to the personal site. | Unchecked: Default location is not restricted. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Global Options\Customize\Shared Workspace\Define Shared Workspace URL's | Site 1: | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\sharepointtracking\name0!name HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\sharepointtracking\name0!url | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Specifies the name and URL of a shared workspace. The name and URL appear in the Document Management pane. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Graph settings | Allow Map Charts to send geographic data to Bing | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\charting!aremapchartsenabled | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting controls whether Map Charts can send geographic data to Bing.  For more information about Map Charts see https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/?linkid=2194000.If you enable this policy setting when a user adds geographic data to a new or existing map chart that data will be sent to Bing and the map chart will be updated to include any new locations.If you disable this policy setting when a user adds geographic data to a new or existing map chart the geographic data won’t be sent to Bing and the map chart won’t be updated to include any new locations. Also the user will see a message letting them know that their map chart can’t be created.If you don’t configure this policy setting when a user adds geographic data to a new or existing map chart that data will be sent to Bing and the map chart will be updated to include any new locations.Note: This policy setting only applies to subscription versions of Office such as Microsoft 365 Apps for enterprise. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Graph settings | Chart Templates Server Location | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\general\charttemplates!sharedchartslocation | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Specifies the location [URL or UNC] for server-based chart templates. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Graph settings | Enable MS Graph as Default Chart Tool in PowerPoint and Word | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\charting!msgraphenable | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Enables administrators to set the default chart creation tool to MS Graph instead of the default Excel Chart in PowerPoint and Word. Also blocks conversion of Graph charts to Office charts. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Graph settings | Graph gallery path | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\graph\options!gallerypath | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Sets the path to store user-defined custom charts. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Help | Federated search for help | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\helpviewer!enablefederatedhelpsearch | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to determine the sources of content that Office users in your organization can access through Office Help (F1).          If you enable or do not configure this policy setting users who utilize Office Help see content from both Office and the Internet.          If you disable this setting Office users’ search results through Help are limited to Office content. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\IME (Japanese) | Set comment fields for Outlook Contacts Dictionary | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\imejp\16.0\wswordcomment\plugins\mapi!fieldsetab | At least Windows Server 2012 Windows 8 or Windows RT | This policy setting allows you to specify the fields to display in the comments in the Outlook Contacts Dictionary. This policy setting applies to Japanese Microsoft IME only.If you enable this policy setting you can specify the fields to display by setting strings which are represented by the following identification letter IDs. You must also use any IDs/strings in the following sequence.ID for each field is as follows:a = Full Namec = Phonetic Namee = Company Namef = Department Nameg = Titleh = Office Locationj = Email Addressk = Business Telephone Numberl = Business AddressFor example if you want to display Full Name Phonetic Name and Company Name specify 'ace'.If you do not configure this policy setting Full Name Phonetic Name Company Name Department Name and Job Title are displayed in the comments in this order. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\IME (Japanese) | Set comment fields for Outlook Global Address List Dictionary | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\imejp\16.0\wswordcomment\plugins\mapi!fieldsetad | At least Windows Server 2012 Windows 8 or Windows RT | This policy setting allows you to specify the fields to display in the comments in the Outlook Global Address List Dictionary. This policy setting applies to Japanese Microsoft IME only.If you enable this policy setting you can specify the fields to display by setting strings which are represented by the following identification letter IDs. You must also use any IDs/strings in the following sequence.The ID for each field is as follows:a = Full Namec = Phonetic Namee = Company Namef = Department Nameg = Titleh = Office Locationj = Email Addressk = Business Telephone Numberl = Business AddressFor example if you want to display Full Name Phonetic Name and Company Name specify 'ace'.If you do not configure this policy setting Full Name Phonetic Name Department Name Job Title and Office Location are displayed in the comments in this order. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\IME (Japanese) | Set update interval for Outlook Contacts Dictionary | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\imejp\16.0\wswordcomment\plugins\mapi!updatespanab | At least Windows Server 2012 Windows 8 or Windows RT | This policy setting allows you to specify the update interval for the Outlook Contacts Dictionary. This policy setting applies to Japanese Microsoft IME only.If you enable this policy setting you can specify the update interval.  For example if you specify "720" the Outlook Contacts Dictionary is updated every 720 minutes.If you do not configure this policy setting the Outlook Contacts Dictionary is updated every 1440 minutes (24 hours). |
| Microsoft Office 2016\IME (Japanese) | Set update interval for Outlook Global Address List Dictionary | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\imejp\16.0\wswordcomment\plugins\mapi!updatespanad | At least Windows Server 2012 Windows 8 or Windows RT | This policy setting allows you to specify the update interval for the Outlook Global Address List Dictionary. This policy setting applies to Japanese Microsoft IME only.If you enable this policy setting you can specify the update interval in minutes.  For example if you specify "720" the Outlook Global Address List Dictionary is updated every 720 minutes.If you do not configure this policy setting the Outlook Global Address List Dictionary is updated every 1440 minutes (24 hours). |
| Microsoft Office 2016\IME (Japanese) | Turn off Outlook name dictionaries update | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\imejp\16.0\wswordcomment\plugins\mapi!disabled | At least Windows Server 2012 Windows 8 or Windows RT | This policy setting allows you to turn off updating for Outlook name dictionaries of Microsoft IME (Input Method Editor). This policy setting applies to Japanese Microsoft IME only.If you enable this policy setting all Outlook name dictionaries are not updated.Outlook name dictionaries that were added before enabling this policy setting are used for conversion.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting Outlook name dictionaries are generated updated and used for conversion. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\IME (Japanese) | Turn off SharePoint dictionary update | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\ime\shared\16.0\sharepointdict!disabled | At least Windows Server 2012 Windows 8 or Windows RT | This policy setting allows you to turn off updating for SharePoint dictionary of Microsoft IME (Input Method Editor). This policy setting applies to Japanese Microsoft IME only.If you enable this policy setting SharePoint dictionary is not updated and you cannot add a new SharePoint dictionary.A SharePoint dictionary that was added before enabling this policy setting is used for conversion.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting SharePoint dictionary can be updated and added. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Improved Error Reporting | Stop reporting error messages | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\alerts!noalertreporting | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting controls whether the application reports error messages.If you enable this policy error messages will not be reported.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting error messages will be reported. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Improved Error Reporting | Stop reporting non-critical errors | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\shipasserts!disableshipasserts | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting controls whether the application reports non-critical errors.If you enable this policy non-critical errors will not be reported.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting non-critical errors will be reported. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Language Preferences | Notify users if they do not have proofing tools for a language they use | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\languageresources!disableproofingtoolsadvertisement | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting allows you to turn on or turn off notifications that are displayed to users when they use a language in their document but do not have proofing tools installed for that language.If you enable this policy setting users see a message bar when they use a language in their document but do not have proofing tools installed for that language. Users cannot disable this notification because the "Never show again" button is not shown and the checkbox to disable proofing notifications is removed from the Options dialog box.If you disable this policy setting users do not see this message bar.If you do not configure this policy setting users see this message bar. They can disable the notification by clicking the "Never show again" button or by clearing a check box in the Options dialog box. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Language Preferences\Display Language | Allow users who aren’t admins to install language accessory packs | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\languageresources!allownonadminuserinstalllanguages | At least Windows Server 2016 Windows 10 | This policy setting controls whether users can install language accessory packs for Office even if they don’t have local administrator permissions on their devices. By default users must have local administrator permissions on their devices to install language accessory packs.If you enable this policy setting users will be able to install language accessory packs for Office even if they don’t have local administrator permissions on their devices. They can install those language accessory packs by going to File > Options > Language in the app such as Word.If you disable or don’t configure this policy setting users who don’t have local administrator permissions on their devices won’t be able to install language accessory packs. Someone with local administrator permissions on the device will need to install the language accessory packs for the user.For more information see https://go.microsoft.com/fwlink/p/?linkid=2161939. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Language Preferences\Display Language | Change or delete link to language pack download site | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\languageresources!disablelanguagepackdownload; HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\languageresources!languagepackdownloadaddress | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting affects the language download link under the display and help language section.If you enable this policy setting you you may enter the URL to another location where language packs may be downloaded.If you disable this policy setting the URL will be removed.If you do not configure this policy setting the URL will remain available and point to the language pack download site on Office.com. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Language Preferences\Display Language | Change or delete link to the proofing tools download site | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\languageresources!disableproofingtoolsdownload; HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\languageresources!proofingtoolsdownloadaddress | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy setting affects the proofing tools link under the Editing language section.If you enable this policy setting you you may enter the URL to another location where proofing tools may be downloaded.If you disable this policy setting the URL will be removed.If you do not configure this policy setting the URL will remain available and point to the proofing tools site on Office.com. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Language Preferences\Display Language | Display help in | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\languageresources!helplanguage | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Sets the default language of online Help.  In addition to configuring this setting consider enabling the same language in the 'Enabled Editing Languages' policy node. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Language Preferences\Display Language | Display menus and dialog boxes in | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\languageresources!uilanguage | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Sets the display language of the user interface for all Office 2016 programs.  In addition to configuring this setting consider enabling the same language in the 'Enabled Editing Languages' policy node. |
| Microsoft Office 2016\Language Preferences\Editing Languages | Primary Editing Language | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\common\languageresources!installlanguage | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Defines the editing options for Office 2016 programs.  In addition to configuring this setting consider enabling the same language in the 'Enabled Editing Languages' policy node.  Please refer to the Office Resource Kit documentation for important information on setting the installed version of Microsoft Office. |
