| Microsoft Word 2016\Word Options\Track changes and compare | Balloons | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\word\options!revmodeballoons | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Turning balloons off will show revisions inline. This corresponds to the choices in the Review ribbon. |
| Microsoft Word 2016\Word Options\Track changes and compare | Compare resulting document | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\word\options!comparedestdoc | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This option determines whether the results of a document compare or combine will appear in a new document or one of the source documents. This corresponds to the option in the Compare dialog box ( Review ribbon | Compare | more options). |
| Microsoft Word 2016\Word Options\Track changes and compare | Deletions color | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\word\options!revmodedelcolor | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Selects the color for tracked deletions. This corresponds to the choices in the Review ribbon | Track changes | Change tracking options dialog box. |
| Microsoft Word 2016\Word Options\Track changes and compare | Ignore White Space | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\word\options!compareignorewhitespace | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This option determines if white space is compared in document compare. This corresponds to the option in the Compare dialog box ( Review ribbon | Compare | more options). |
| Microsoft Word 2016\Word Options\Track changes and compare | Insertions color | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\word\options!revmodeinscolor | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Selects the default color for tracked insertions. This corresponds to the choices in the Review ribbon | Track changes | Change tracking options dialog box. |
| Microsoft Word 2016\Word Options\Track changes and compare | Table compare colors | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\word\options!revmodeinscellcolor HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\word\options!revmodedelcellcolor HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\word\options!revmodemergecellcolor HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\word\options!revmodesplitcellcolor | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This option determines the colors used for displaying the results of compared tables.  Selecting ''none'' will track the changes but they will not be colored in the resulting document (they will be listed in the reviewing pane). |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Additional server versions supported | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!configuredservercheckvalues | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Specify a semicolon separated list of server version names e.g. RTC/2.9;RTC/3.0;RTC/4.0 to which Microsoft Lync allows logon in addition to the server versions that are supported by default. Space character is treated as part of the version string. |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Allow storage of user passwords | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!savepassword | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Allows Microsoft Lync to store user passwords.If you enable this policy setting Microsoft Lync can store a password on request from the user.If you disable this policy setting Microsoft Lync cannot store a password.If you do not configure this policy setting and the user logs on to a domain Microsoft Lync does not store the password. If you do not configure this policy setting and the user does not log on to a domain (for example if the user logs on to a workgroup) Microsoft Lync can store the password.Note: You can configure this policy setting under both Computer Configuration and User Configuration but the policy setting under Computer Configuration takes precedence. |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Configure SIP compression mode | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!sipcompression | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Defines when to turn on SIP compression. Default: Based on adaptor speed.Setting this policy may cause an increase in sign-in time. |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Configure SIP security mode | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!enablesiphighsecuritymode | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | When Lync connects to the server it supports various authentication mechanisms.  This policy allows the user to specify whether Digest and Basic authentication are supported.Disabled (default):  NTLM/Kerberos/TLS-DSK/Digest/BasicEnabled:  Authentication mechanisms:  NTLM/Kerberos/TLS-DSK  Gal Download: Requires HTTPS if user is not logged in as an internal user. |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Disable automatic upload of sign-in failure logs | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!disableautomaticsendtracing | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Uploads the sign-in failure logs to the Microsoft Lync Server automatically for analysis.  No logs will be automatically uploaded if sign-in is successful.If this policy is not configured then the following happens: For Lync Online Users:  Sign-in failure logs are automatically uploaded.For Lync On-Premise Users:  A confirmation seeking consent from the user is shown before upload.When this is disabled sign-in logs would be uploaded to the Microsoft Lync Server for both Lync On-Premise and Online users automatically.When this is enabled sign-in logs will never be uploaded automatically. |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Disable HTTP fallback for SIP connection | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!disablehttpconnect | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Prevents from HTTP being used for SIP connection in case TLS or TCP fail. |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Disable server version check | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!disableservercheck | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Prevents Microsoft Lync from checking the server version before signing in. |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Enable using BITS to download Address Book Service files | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!enablebitsforgaldownload | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | This policy allows Microsoft Lync to use BITS (Background Intelligent Transfer Service) to download the Address Book Services files. |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Global Address Book Download Initial Delay | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!galdownloadinitialdelay | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | When set this will delay the initial download of the Global Address Book by a random number between 0 and the number of minutes specified after sign-in.  When the value is 0 the download will begin immediately after sign-in.  By default the value is 60.  This means that there will be a random delay between 0 and 60 minutes after sign-in before Lync begins to download the address book. |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Prevent users from running Microsoft Lync | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!preventrun | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Prevents users from running Microsoft Lync.If you enable this policy setting users cannot run Microsoft Lync.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting users can run Microsoft Lync.Note: You can configure this policy setting under both Computer Configuration and User Configuration but the policy setting under Computer Configuration takes precedence. |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Require logon credentials | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!disablentcredentials | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Requires the user to provide logon credentials for Microsoft Lync rather than automatically using the Windows credentials when Microsoft Lync authenticates the user using NTLM or Kerberos.If you enable this policy setting Microsoft Lync requires the user to provide logon credentials.If you disable or do not configure this policy setting Microsoft Lync authenticates the user based on the logon credentials for Windows.Note: You can configure this policy setting under both Computer Configuration and User Configuration but the policy setting under Computer Configuration takes precedence. |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Specify server | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!configurationmode; HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!serveraddressinternal HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!serveraddressexternal | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | Specifies how Microsoft Lync identifies the server.If you enable this policy setting you must specify the server name that Microsoft Lync uses.If you disable this policy setting Microsoft Lync uses a DNS lookup to identify the server.If you do not configure this policy setting the user can choose automatic configuration or the user can specify the name of the server in Microsoft Lync user preferences. To set the user preferences from the Microsoft Lync Tools menu click Options click the Personal tab within the SIP Communications My Account area click Advanced select Configure Settings type the server name in the Server name field.Note: You can configure this policy setting under both Computer Configuration and User Configuration but the policy setting under Computer Configuration takes precedence. |
| Skype for Business 2016\Microsoft Lync Feature Policies | Trusted Domain List | NaN | NaN | NaN | HKCU\software\policies\microsoft\office\16.0\lync!trustmodeldata | At least Windows Server 2008 R2 or Windows 7 | When Lync connects to an unknown domain it needs explicit user consent.  A dialog is shown asking the user for confirmation on whether it should continue.This policy gives administrators the ability to provide trusted domain names.  If a domain name is added to this list Lync will trust that domain and will not show the dialog requesting permission.  Multiple domain addresses as comma separated values can be provided.By setting this policy Lync will not explicitly trust the default domains specified below.  It will exclusively trust the domain specified by the policy.Supported values:  Not Configured (Default)/Disabled:  By default the following domains will be trusted:  "lync.com outlook.com lync.glbdns.microsoft.com and microsoftonline.com."  Enabled:  The list of domains to be trusted.  For example: "contoso.com contoso.co.in" |
